text: Decide whether optional packages should stay in the root npm workspace install set
description: The default release story should not force unnecessary dependencies, but MCP is strategically important while voice is explicitly optional. Decide whether optional packages like voice-bridge and mcp should remain in the root workspace install set or move to separate opt-in installs. This decision affects npm install behavior, scripts, contributor ergonomics, and how strongly optional integrations are separated from the core local-first path.

reflections.context: Decide whether optional packages like MCP and voice-bridge should stay in the root workspace install set or be moved to separate opt-in installs.
reflections.outcome: Verified the current implementation: packages remain in workspace (installed by npm install) but are excluded from default dev/build/start commands. They're opt-in via dev:full and build:optional scripts. This represents a deliberate middle-ground decision.
reflections.effectiveness: This approach balances contributor ergonomics (single workspace, no complex install docs) with first-run simplicity (default commands only start core services). The package.json clearly separates core vs optional via build:core and build:optional scripts.
reflections.lesson: For monorepo packaging decisions, verify the current implementation before assuming the decision is unresolved. Often the code already represents the chosen strategy and just needs documentation.
reflections.pattern: When an 'unclear' aim has an existing working implementation that matches the design goals, mark it done with documentation of the chosen approach rather than treating it as blocked.

status.comment: Decision made: optional packages (mcp, voice-bridge) stay in workspace for contributor ergonomics, but are excluded from default npm run dev/build/start commands. They're opt-in via dev:full and build:optional. This balances contributor convenience with first-run simplicity.
